prose:
Method–provenance summary

Property: HOMO–LUMO gap of gas-phase H₂O (eV)
Level of theory: density-fitted DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard”; RI/JK)
Geometry: fixed Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz (no re-optimization → single-point DFT//input-geometry)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet; RKS)
Software/backend: PySCF via chemkit “frontier-orbitals” skill
SCF convergence: converged in 7 cycles (scf_converged: true)
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine
Live .out log: chemkit streamed stdout/stderr to a frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory (path printed at launch and available for inspection)

Headline numerical result (direct from chemkit)
HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV
 HOMO energy = –8.6037183 eV
 LUMO energy =  +0.33695037 eV

Caveats
1. Orbital energies and their difference constitute a Koopmans (independent-particle) gap; they are not correlated excitation energies.
2. Density fitting (RI/JK) is an additional approximation; it is PySCF’s default for this functional/basis.
3. Gas-phase, single-conformer result — no solvation or temperature corrections included.

All numbers and metadata are reproduced exactly as returned by the chemkit engine; no rounding beyond the tool’s own output has been applied.
